items.title: Justifying non-linear models over linear baselines
items.description: When tabular or sensor data shows clear non-linear structure (curves, thresholds, interactions), UAT intuition supports using an MLP instead of assuming linearity is a fundamental limit — the hypothesis class can represent rich continuous maps on bounded features.

items.title: Architecture exploration for regression and classification
items.description: Teams compare shallow-wide vs deeper MLPs knowing both can approximate in principle; they pick based on validation error, parameter count, and training stability rather than treating depth as mandatory or forbidden by theory.

items.title: Teaching and interview framing
items.description: Engineers explain why hidden layers exist (XOR, non-linear boundaries) and cite UAT as the formal expressivity backstop — while immediately pairing it with generalization and regularization practices for production systems.

items.title: Simulation and control surrogate modeling
items.description: Physics engines and PDE solvers are expensive; MLP surrogates approximate continuous input–output maps on bounded operating regions. UAT supports feasibility; held-out simulation runs validate accuracy before deployment.
